content.text:
You are applying a code migration fix.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/components/common/FormGroupWithHelpText/FormGroupWithHelpText.tsx
Line: 73

Migration rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   72          <HelperText>
   73            <HelperTextItem
   74              variant={variant}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/components/common/FormGroupWithHelpText/FormGroupWithHelpText.tsx
2. Apply ONLY the change described by the migration rule at or near line 73
3. Make the minimum edit necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your change (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Write the fixed file
6. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through the fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edit, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists the change you made, or note if the fix was already applied or could not be applied (with a brief reason).

1 import type { FC, ReactNode } from 'react'; 2 3 import { 4 FormGroup, 5 type FormGroupProps, 6 FormHelperText, 7 HelperText, 8 HelperTextItem, 9 } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 10 11 type FormGroupWithHelpTextProps = { 12 /** 13 * Sets the FormGroup validated. If you set to success, text color of helper text will be modified to indicate valid state. 14 * If set to error, text color of helper text will be modified to indicate error state. 15 * If set to warning, text color of helper text will be modified to indicate warning state. 16 */ 17 validated?: 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'default'; 18 /** 19 * Helper text regarding the field. It can be a simple text or an object. 20 */ 21 helperText?: ReactNode; 22 /** 23 * Helper text after the field when the field is invalid. It can be a simple text or an object. 24 */ 25 helperTextInvalid?: ReactNode; 26 } & FormGroupProps; 27 28 /** 29 * Convert the formGroup validated mode into the variant styling of the helper text item 30 * If validated mode was not set or if it's the 'default', set the variant styling to 'indeterminate' 31 * for being consistent with PF4 behavior 32 */ 33 const validatedToVariant = (validated: 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'default' | undefined) => 34 !validated || validated === 'default' ? 'indeterminate' : validated; 35 36 /** 37 * A FormGroup component that supports helperTexts 38 * 39 * This component wraps the FormGroup with an option to use the following helper text related properties 40 * (since not supported anymore as part of the FormGroup component in PatternFly 5): 41 * helperText, helperTextInvalid, validated 42 * 43 * `See` https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/#helper-text 44 * 45 * [ 46 * View component source on GitHub](https://github.com/kubev2v/forklift-console-plugin/blob/main/packages/common/src/components/FormGroupWithHelpText/FormGroupWithHelpText.tsx) 47 */ 48 export const FormGroupWithHelpText: FC = ({ 49 children, 50 fieldId, 51 helperText, 52 helperTextInvalid, 53 isRequired, 54 label, 55 labelIcon, 56 role, 57 validated, 58 }) => { 59 const helperTextMsg = validated === 'error' && helperTextInvalid ? helperTextInvalid : helperText; 60 const variant = validatedToVariant(validated); 61 62 return ( 63 70 {children} 71 72 73 77 {helperTextMsg} 78 79 80 81 82 ); 83 };

structuredContent.stdout: 1 import type { FC, ReactNode } from 'react'; 2 3 import { 4 FormGroup, 5 type FormGroupProps, 6 FormHelperText, 7 HelperText, 8 HelperTextItem, 9 } from '@patternfly/react-core'; 10 11 type FormGroupWithHelpTextProps = { 12 /** 13 * Sets the FormGroup validated. If you set to success, text color of helper text will be modified to indicate valid state. 14 * If set to error, text color of helper text will be modified to indicate error state. 15 * If set to warning, text color of helper text will be modified to indicate warning state. 16 */ 17 validated?: 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'default'; 18 /** 19 * Helper text regarding the field. It can be a simple text or an object. 20 */ 21 helperText?: ReactNode; 22 /** 23 * Helper text after the field when the field is invalid. It can be a simple text or an object. 24 */ 25 helperTextInvalid?: ReactNode; 26 } & FormGroupProps; 27 28 /** 29 * Convert the formGroup validated mode into the variant styling of the helper text item 30 * If validated mode was not set or if it's the 'default', set the variant styling to 'indeterminate' 31 * for being consistent with PF4 behavior 32 */ 33 const validatedToVariant = (validated: 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'default' | undefined) => 34 !validated || validated === 'default' ? 'indeterminate' : validated; 35 36 /** 37 * A FormGroup component that supports helperTexts 38 * 39 * This component wraps the FormGroup with an option to use the following helper text related properties 40 * (since not supported anymore as part of the FormGroup component in PatternFly 5): 41 * helperText, helperTextInvalid, validated 42 * 43 * `See` https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/#helper-text 44 * 45 * [ 46 * View component source on GitHub](https://github.com/kubev2v/forklift-console-plugin/blob/main/packages/common/src/components/FormGroupWithHelpText/FormGroupWithHelpText.tsx) 47 */ 48 export const FormGroupWithHelpText: FC = ({ 49 children, 50 fieldId, 51 helperText, 52 helperTextInvalid, 53 isRequired, 54 label, 55 labelIcon, 56 role, 57 validated, 58 }) => { 59 const helperTextMsg = validated === 'error' && helperTextInvalid ? helperTextInvalid : helperText; 60 const variant = validatedToVariant(validated); 61 62 return ( 63 70 {children} 71 72 73 77 {helperTextMsg} 78 79 80 81 82 ); 83 };

content.text:
Now let me analyze the code:

**Line 73-78**: The `<HelperTextItem>` has `{helperTextMsg}` as its children. `helperTextMsg` is a `ReactNode` that comes from either `helperTextInvalid` or `helperText` props, both typed as `ReactNode`.

**Migration rule**: The rule says to move icon content from children to the `icon` prop. However, looking at this code:

1. `helperTextMsg` is a dynamic `ReactNode` — it could be anything (text, icon, or a mix). We can't statically determine at compile time whether it contains an icon.
2. The `helperTextMsg` is general-purpose helper text content, not specifically an icon element.

The migration rule fires at line 73 because the scanner detected a `HelperTextItem` component. However, looking at the actual children (`{helperTextMsg}`), this is dynamic content passed in by consumers of this component. The content is a `ReactNode` that could be plain text strings (based on the prop names `helperText` and `helperTextInvalid`).

There's no static icon element being passed as children here — `helperTextMsg` is a runtime value. There's no icon import from `@patternfly/react-icons` in this file. The children content is semantically "helper text" (not an icon), so this rule doesn't apply to this specific usage.

Also, I notice line 67 references `labelHelp` but the destructured prop is `labelIcon`. Let me check if that's a pre-existing issue or relevant.

Looking more carefully: the prop destructured is `labelIcon` (line 55) but used as `labelHelp` (line 67). That's a separate issue — likely a different migration rule already renamed it, or it's a bug. I should NOT touch that since the instruction says to apply ONLY the change described by this migration rule.

**Conclusion**: The `HelperTextItem` at line 73 does not have any icon element as children — it has `{helperTextMsg}` which is a `ReactNode` containing helper text. The migration rule about moving icon children to the `icon` prop does not apply here because there is no icon to move.

## Changes Applied

**No change applied.** The `<HelperTextItem>` at line 73 does not contain any icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons` as children. Its children (`{helperTextMsg}`) is a dynamic `ReactNode` containing helper text content (from the `helperText` or `helperTextInvalid` props). There is no static icon to move to the `icon` prop, so this migration rule does not apply to this usage.
